canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, data diversity is commonly defined as the extent of nonredundant and informative variation within a dataset. One framing specifies this variation relative to what a model has already learned, while another describes it more broadly as a latent characteristic reflecting the "breadth and variability of its content." The construct is operationalized through mechanisms described as "diversity-checkers." As one study explains, a low value from such a checker indicates a data point is less redundant and contains more information, thus contributing to higher diversity. This concept is applied in the context of evaluating both LLM-generated text and multimodal data. Its role is also contrasted with data quality, with one paper proposing that data diversity has a more pronounced effect for image-to-text models compared to text-to-video or image-text pre-training models.
expert_removed_reason: data property / engineering — not a construct of LLM ability
papers: Not All LLM-Generated Data Are Equal: Rethinking Data Weighting in Text Classification
Revisit Large-Scale Image-Caption Data in Pre-training Multimodal Foundation Models
Data-Juicer Sandbox: A Feedback-Driven Suite for Multimodal Data-Model Co-development
The Best of Both Worlds: Bridging Quality and Diversity in Data Selection with Bipartite Graph

definitions.text: The extent to which examples provide nonredundant, informative variation within a dataset or relative to what a model has already learned.
definitions.paper: Not All LLM-Generated Data Are Equal: Rethinking Data Weighting in Text Classification
